name: Ade Bench
desc: Analytics Data Engineer Bench: tasks evaluating AI agents on dbt/SQL data analytics engineering bugs.

name: AgentBench
desc: A benchmark designed to evaluate LLMs as Agents

name: Aider Polyglot
desc: A polyglot coding benchmark that evaluates AI agents' ability to perform code editing and generation tasks across multiple programming languages.

name: Algotune
desc: AlgoTune: 154 algorithm optimization tasks focusing on speedup-based scoring from the AlgoTune benchmark.

name: APPS
desc: APPS is a dataset for evaluating model performance on Python programming tasks across three difficulty levels consisting of 1,000 at introductory, 3,000 at interview, and 1,000 at competition level.

name: AutoCodeBench
desc: Multilingual automated code generation benchmark evaluating LLMs across diverse programming tasks and languages.

name: BigCodeBench
desc: Python coding benchmark with 1,140 diverse questions drawing on numerous python libraries.

name: Bigcodebench Hard Complete
desc: BigCodeBench-Hard complete benchmark adapter for Harbor - challenging Python programming tasks with reward-based verification.

name: Bird Bench
desc: BIRD SQL parity subset (150 tasks, seed 42).

name: ClassEval
desc: Evaluates LLMs on class-level code generation with 100 tasks constructed over 500 person-hours.

name: Code Contests
desc: A competitive programming benchmark from DeepMind that evaluates AI agents' ability to solve algorithmic problems, covering algorithms, data structures, and competitive programming challenges.

name: Compilebench
desc: Version 1.0 of CompileBench, a benchmark on real open-source projects against dependency hell, legacy toolchains, and complex build systems.

name: ComputeEval
desc: Evaluates LLM capability to generate correct CUDA code for kernel implementation, memory management, and parallel algorithm optimization tasks.

name: Cooperbench
desc: CooperBench: multi-agent cooperation benchmark. 652 feature pairs across 12 repos requiring two agents to coordinate via messaging.

name: Crustbench
desc: CRUST-bench: 100 C-to-safe-Rust transpilation tasks from real-world C repositories.

name: Deveval
desc: DevEval benchmark: comprehensive evaluation of LLMs across software development lifecycle (implementation, unit testing, acceptance testing) for 21 real-world repositories across Python, C++, Java, and JavaScript.

name: Ds 1000
desc: DS-1000 is a code generation benchmark with 1000 realistic data science problems across seven popular Python libraries.

name: DS-1000
desc: Code generation benchmark with a thousand data science problems spanning seven Python libraries.

name: Evoeval
desc: EvoEval_difficult: 100 challenging Python programming tasks evolved from HumanEval.

name: Featurebench
desc: FeatureBench full split: 200 feature-implementation tasks across 24 Python repos. 7 tasks require Ampere+ GPU.

name: Featurebench Lite
desc: FeatureBench lite split: 30 feature-implementation tasks (26 lv1 + 4 lv2) across Python repos.

name: Featurebench Lite Modal
desc: FeatureBench lite split for Modal: 30 feature-implementation tasks with gpus=1 for GPU tasks (7/30). Use with -e modal.

name: Featurebench Modal
desc: FeatureBench full split for Modal: 200 feature-implementation tasks with gpus=1 for GPU tasks (44/200). Use with -e modal.

name: Frontier-CS
desc: 238 open-ended computer science problems spanning algorithmic (172) and research (66) tracks.

name: Gso
desc: GSO: 102 software optimization tasks focusing on performance improvement.

name: Hello World
desc: A simple example task to create a hello.txt file with 'Hello, world!' as content.

name: HumanEval
desc: Assesses how accurately language models can write correct Python functions based solely on natural-language instructions provided as docstrings.

name: Humanevalfix
desc: HumanEvalFix: 164 Python code repair tasks from HumanEvalPack.

name: IFEvalCode
desc: Evaluates code generation models on their ability to produce correct code while adhering to specific instruction constraints across 8 programming languages.

name: KernelBench
desc: A benchmark for evaluating the ability of LLMs to write efficient GPU kernels.

name: Legacy Bench
desc: A benchmark for evaluating AI agents on legacy code maintenance and modernization tasks across multiple language families including COBOL, Java 7, C, Fortran, and Assembly.

name: Livecodebench
desc: A subset of 100 sampled tasks from the release_v6 version of LiveCodeBench tasks.

name: LiveCodeBench-Pro
desc: Evaluates LLMs on competitive programming problems using a specialized Docker sandbox (LightCPVerifier) to execute and judge C++ code submissions against hidden test cases with time and memory constraints.

name: MBPP
desc: Measures the ability of language models to generate short Python programs from simple natural-language descriptions, testing basic coding proficiency.

name: Ml Dev Bench
desc: ML-Dev-Bench: A benchmark for testing AI agents on machine learning development tasks including model implementation, training, debugging, and optimization.

name: MLE-bench
desc: Machine learning tasks drawn from 75 Kaggle competitions.

name: Mlgym Bench
desc: Evaluates agents on ML tasks across computer vision, RL, tabular ML, and game theory.

name: MLRC-Bench
desc: This benchmark evaluates LLM-based research agents on their ability to propose and implement novel methods using tasks from recent ML conference competitions, assessing both novelty and effectiveness compared to a baseline and top human solutions.

name: Openthoughts Tblite
desc: OpenThoughts-TBLite: A difficulty-calibrated benchmark of 100 tasks for building terminal agents. By OpenThoughts Agent team, Snorkel AI, Bespoke Labs.

name: Otel Bench
desc: OpenTelemetry Benchmark - evaluates AI agents' ability to instrument applications with OpenTelemetry tracing across multiple languages.

name: PaperBench
desc: Agents are evaluated on their ability to replicate 20 ICML 2024 Spotlight and Oral papers from scratch.

name: Quixbugs
desc: QuixBugs is a multi-lingual program repair benchmark with 40 Python and 40 Java programs, each containing a single-line defect. Tasks cover algorithms and data structures including sorting, graph, dynamic programming, math, and string/array operations.

name: Researchcodebench
desc: ResearchCodeBench evaluates AI agents' ability to implement algorithms from academic papers. Contains 212 code implementation tasks across 20 ML/AI research problems from top-tier venues (ICLR, NeurIPS, CVPR, COLM). Tests paper comprehension, algorithm understanding, and precise code implementation skills with 1,449 lines of reference code.

name: Rexbench
desc: A benchmark to evaluate the ability of AI agents to extend existing AI research through research experiment implementation tasks.

name: Seta Env
desc: CAMEL SETA Environment for RL training.

name: Spider2 Dbt
desc: Spider 2.0-DBT is a comprehensive code generation agent task that includes 68 examples. Solving these tasks requires models to understand project code, navigating complex SQL environments and handling long contexts, surpassing traditional text-to-SQL challenges.

name: Swe Atlas Qna
desc: SWE-Atlas Codebase QnA benchmark that evaluates AI agents' ability to comprehend and query existing codebases.

name: Swe Atlas Tw
desc: SWE-Atlas Test Writing benchmark that evaluates AI agents' ability to write comprehensive unit tests.

name: Swe Gen Js
desc: SWE-gen-JS: 1000 JavaScript/TypeScript bug fix tasks from 30 open-source GitHub repos, generated using SWE-gen.

name: Swe Lancer Diamond
desc: Both manager and individual contributor tasks.

name: Swe Lancer Diamond
desc: Only the individual contributor SWE tasks.

name: Swe Lancer Diamond
desc: Only the manager tasks.

name: SWE-bench Verified
desc: Evaluates AI's ability to resolve genuine software engineering issues sourced from 12 popular Python GitHub repositories, reflecting realistic coding and debugging scenarios.

name: SWE-Lancer
desc: A benchmark of freelance software engineering tasks from Upwork, valued at $1 million USD total in realworld payouts.

name: Swebench Multilingual
desc: SWE-bench Multilingual extends the original Python-focused SWE-bench benchmark to support multiple programming languages.

name: Swebench Verified
desc: A human-validated subset of 500 SWE-bench tasks.

name: Swebenchpro
desc: SWE-bench Pro: A multi-language software engineering benchmark with 731 instances covering Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, and Go. Evaluates AI systems' ability to resolve real-world bugs and implement features across diverse production codebases.

name: Swesmith
desc: SWE-smith is a synthetically generated dataset of software engineering tasks derived from GitHub issues for training and evaluating code generation models.

name: Swtbench Verified
desc: SWTBench Verified - Software Testing Benchmark for code generation.

name: Termigen Environments
desc: 3,500+ verified Docker environments for training and evaluating terminal agents, spanning 11 task categories across infrastructure, data/algorithm applications, and specialized domains including software build, system administration, security, data processing, ML/MLOps, algorithms, scientific computing, and more.

name: Terminal Bench
desc: Version 2.0 of Terminal-Bench, a benchmark for testing agents in terminal environments. More tasks, harder, and higher quality than 1.0.

name: Terminal Bench Pro
desc: Terminal-Bench Pro (Public Set) is an extended benchmark dataset for testing AI agents in real terminal environments. From compiling code to training models and setting up servers, Terminal-Bench Pro evaluates how well agents can handle real-world, end-to-end tasks autonomously.

name: Terminal Bench Sample
desc: A sample of tasks from Terminal-Bench 2.0.

name: USACO
desc: Evaluates language model performance on difficult Olympiad programming problems across four difficulty levels.

name: Usaco
desc: USACO: 304 Python programming problems from USACO competition.

name: Vmax Tasks
desc: A collection of 1,043 validated real-world bug-fixing tasks from popular open-source JavaScript projects including Vue.js, Docusaurus, Redux, and Chalk. Each task presents an authentic bug report with reproduction steps and expected behavior.

name: AssistantBench
desc: Tests whether AI agents can perform real-world time-consuming tasks on the web.

name: BFCL
desc: Evaluates LLM function/tool-calling ability on a simplified split of the Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard (BFCL).

name: Bfcl
desc: Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard: 3,641 function calling tasks for evaluating LLM tool use capabilities across simple, multiple, parallel, and irrelevance categories.

name: Bfcl Parity
desc: BFCL parity subset: 123 stratified sampled tasks for validating Harbor adapter equivalence with original BFCL benchmark.

name: BrowseComp
desc: A benchmark for evaluating agents' ability to browse the web.

name: GAIA
desc: Proposes real-world questions that require a set of fundamental abilities such as reasoning, multi-modality handling, web browsing, and generally tool-use proficiency.

name: Gaia
desc: GAIA (General AI Assistants): 165 validation tasks for multi-step reasoning, tool use, and multimodal question answering.

name: Mind2Web
desc: A dataset for developing and evaluating generalist agents for the web that can follow language instructions to complete complex tasks on any website.

name: OSWorld
desc: Tests AI agents' ability to perform realistic, open-ended tasks within simulated computer environments, requiring complex interaction across multiple input modalities.

name: Sycophancy Eval
desc: Evaluate sycophancy of language models across a variety of free-form text-generation tasks.

name: Tau2
desc: Evaluating Conversational Agents in a Dual-Control Environment

name: The Agent Company
desc: The Agent Company benchmark evaluates autonomous agents in a realistic, self-contained company environment.

name: Arc Agi 2
desc: ARC-AGI-2: A benchmark measuring abstract reasoning through visual grid puzzles requiring rule inference and generalization.

name: BBH
desc: Tests AI models on a suite of 23 challenging BIG-Bench tasks that previously proved difficult even for advanced language models to solve.

name: BIG-Bench Extra Hard
desc: A reasoning capability dataset that replaces each task in BIG-Bench-Hard with a novel task that probes a similar reasoning capability but exhibits significantly increased difficulty.

name: BoolQ
desc: Reading comprehension dataset that queries for complex, non-factoid information, and require difficult entailment-like inference to solve.

name: DROP
desc: Evaluates reading comprehension where models must resolve references in a question, perhaps to multiple input positions, and perform discrete operations over them (such as addition, counting, or sorting).

name: HellaSwag
desc: Tests models' commonsense reasoning abilities by asking them to select the most likely next step or continuation for a given everyday situation.

name: IFEval
desc: Evaluates how well language models can strictly follow detailed instructions, such as writing responses with specific word counts or including required keywords.

name: Kumo
desc: KUMO full dataset (5300 tasks; 50 instances per scenario).

name: Kumo
desc: KUMO(easy) split (5050 tasks; 50 instances per scenario).

name: Kumo
desc: KUMO(hard) split (250 tasks; 50 instances per scenario).

name: Kumo
desc: KUMO parity subset (seeds 0/1; 212 tasks).

name: LingOly
desc: Two linguistics reasoning benchmarks: LingOly (Linguistic Olympiad questions) is a benchmark utilising low resource languages.

name: MMMU
desc: Assesses multimodal AI models on challenging college-level questions covering multiple academic subjects, requiring detailed visual interpretation, in-depth reasoning, and both multiple-choice and open-ended answering abilities.

name: MuSR
desc: Evaluating models on multistep soft reasoning tasks in the form of free text narratives.

name: Needle in a Haystack (NIAH)
desc: NIAH evaluates in-context retrieval ability of long context LLMs by testing a model's ability to extract factual information from long-context inputs.

name: NoveltyBench
desc: Evaluates how well language models generate diverse, humanlike responses across multiple reasoning and generation tasks.

name: PAWS
desc: Evaluating models on the task of paraphrase detection by providing pairs of sentences that are either paraphrases or not.

name: PIQA
desc: Measures the model's ability to apply practical, everyday commonsense reasoning about physical objects and scenarios through simple decision-making questions.

name: RACE-H
desc: Reading comprehension tasks collected from the English exams for middle and high school Chinese students in the age range between 12 to 18.

name: Reasoning Gym Easy
desc: Reasoning Gym benchmark (easy difficulty).

name: Reasoning Gym Hard
desc: Reasoning Gym benchmark (hard difficulty).

name: Satbench
desc: SATBench is a benchmark for evaluating the logical reasoning capabilities of LLMs through logical puzzles derived from Boolean satisfiability (SAT) problems.

name: SQuAD
desc: Set of 100,000+ questions posed by crowdworkers on a set of Wikipedia articles, where the answer to each question is a segment of text from the corresponding reading passage.

name: VimGolf
desc: A benchmark that evaluates LLMs in their ability to operate Vim editor and complete editing challenges.

name: WINOGRANDE
desc: Set of 273 expert-crafted pronoun resolution problems originally designed to be unsolvable for statistical models that rely on selectional preferences or word associations.

name: WorldSense
desc: Measures grounded reasoning over synthetic world descriptions while controlling for dataset bias.

name: WritingBench
desc: A comprehensive evaluation benchmark designed to assess large language models' capabilities across diverse writing tasks.

name: ∞Bench
desc: LLM benchmark featuring an average data length surpassing 100K tokens.

name: AGIEval
desc: AGIEval is a human-centric benchmark specifically designed to evaluate the general abilities of foundation models in tasks pertinent to human cognition and problem-solving.

name: BBQ
desc: A dataset for evaluating bias in question answering models across multiple social dimensions.

name: BOLD
desc: A dataset to measure fairness in open-ended text generation, covering five domains: profession, gender, race, religious ideologies, and political ideologies.

name: CommonsenseQA
desc: Evaluates an AI model's ability to correctly answer everyday questions that rely on basic commonsense knowledge and understanding of the world.

name: Humanity's Last Exam
desc: Humanity's Last Exam (HLE) is a multi-modal benchmark at the frontier of human knowledge, designed to be the final closed-ended academic benchmark of its kind with broad subject coverage.

name: LiveBench
desc: LiveBench is a benchmark designed with test set contamination and objective evaluation in mind by releasing new questions regularly, as well as having questions based on recently-released datasets.

name: MMLU
desc: Evaluate models on 57 tasks including elementary mathematics, US history, computer science, law, and more.

name: MMLU-Pro
desc: An advanced benchmark that tests both broad knowledge and reasoning capabilities across many subjects, featuring challenging questions and multiple-choice answers with increased difficulty and complexity.

name: Mmmlu
desc: MMMLU (Multilingual MMLU) parity validation subset with 10 tasks per language across 15 languages (150 tasks total). Evaluates language models' subject knowledge and reasoning across multiple languages using multiple-choice questions covering 57 academic subjects.

name: O-NET
desc: Questions and answers from the Ordinary National Educational Test (O-NET), administered annually by the National Institute of Educational Testing Service to Matthayom 6 (Grade 12 / ISCED 3) students in Thailand.

name: Personality
desc: An evaluation suite consisting of multiple personality tests that can be applied to LLMs.

name: Simpleqa
desc: SimpleQA: 4,326 short, fact-seeking questions from OpenAI for evaluating language model factuality. Uses LLM-as-a-judge grading.

name: SimpleQA/SimpleQA Verified
desc: A benchmark that evaluates the ability of language models to answer short, fact-seeking questions.

name: TruthfulQA
desc: Measure whether a language model is truthful in generating answers to questions using questions that some humans would answer falsely due to a false belief or misconception.

name: XSTest
desc: Dataset with 250 safe prompts across ten prompt types that well-calibrated models should not refuse, and 200 unsafe prompts as contrasts that models, for most applications, should refuse.

name: Binary Audit
desc: An open-source benchmark for evaluating AI agents' ability to find backdoors hidden in compiled binaries.

name: Catastrophic Cyber Capabilities Benchmark (3CB)
desc: A benchmark for evaluating the capabilities of LLM agents in cyber offense.

name: CTI-REALM
desc: Evaluates AI systems' ability to analyze cyber threat intelligence and develop comprehensive detection capabilities through a realistic 5-subtask workflow: MITRE technique mapping, data source discovery, Sigma rule generation, KQL development and testing against real telemetry data, and results analysis.

name: CVEBench
desc: Characterises an AI Agent's capability to exploit real-world web application vulnerabilities.

name: Cybench
desc: Tests language models on cybersecurity skills using 39 of 40 practical, professional-level challenges taken from cybersecurity competitions, designed to cover various difficulty levels and security concepts.

name: CyberGym
desc: A large-scale, high-quality cybersecurity evaluation framework designed to rigorously assess the capabilities of AI agents on real-world vulnerability analysis tasks.

name: CyberMetric
desc: Datasets containing 80, 500, 2000 and 10000 multiple-choice questions, designed to evaluate understanding across nine domains within cybersecurity

name: CYBERSECEVAL 3
desc: Evaluates Large Language Models for cybersecurity risk to third parties, application developers and end users.

name: CyberSecEval_2
desc: Assesses language models for cybersecurity risks, specifically testing their potential to misuse programming interpreters, vulnerability to malicious prompt injections, and capability to exploit known software vulnerabilities.

name: GDM Dangerous Capabilities
desc: CTF challenges covering web app vulnerabilities, off-the-shelf exploits, databases, Linux privilege escalation, password cracking and spraying.

name: InterCode
desc: Tests AI's ability in coding, cryptography, reverse engineering, and vulnerability identification through practical capture-the-flag (CTF) cybersecurity scenarios.

name: SecQA
desc: "Security Question Answering" dataset to assess LLMs' understanding and application of security principles.

name: SEvenLLM
desc: Designed for analyzing cybersecurity incidents, which is comprised of two primary task categories: understanding and generation, with a further breakdown into 28 subcategories of tasks.

name: AbstentionBench
desc: Evaluating abstention across 20 diverse datasets, including questions with unknown answers, underspecification, false premises, subjective interpretations, and outdated information.

name: AgentDojo
desc: Assesses whether AI agents can be hijacked by malicious third parties using prompt injections in simple environments such as a workspace or travel booking app.

name: AgentHarm
desc: Assesses whether AI agents might engage in harmful activities by testing their responses to malicious prompts in areas like cybercrime, harassment, and fraud, aiming to ensure safe behavior.

name: AHB
desc: Evaluates how models consider the welfare of animals in situations that may present them harm.

name: APE
desc: Measures a model's willingness to attempt persuasion on harmful, controversial, and benign topics.

name: b3
desc: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LLMs for agentic AI security vulnerabilities including prompt attacks aimed at data exfiltration, content injection, decision and behavior manipulation, denial of service, system and tool compromise, and content policy bypass.

name: CodeIPI
desc: Measures coding agent vulnerability to indirect prompt injection attacks embedded in software engineering artifacts (issue descriptions, code comments, README files).

name: FORTRESS
desc: A dataset of 500 expert-crafted adversarial prompts with instance-based rubrics of 4-7 binary questions for automated evaluation across 3 domains relevant to national security and public safety (NSPS).

name: Make Me Pay
desc: Evaluates an AI models' susceptibility to social engineering attacks by testing whether a "con-artist" model can persuade a "mark" model to donate money through manipulation and persuasion tactics.

name: MakeMeSay
desc: Tests a model’s ability to generate persuasive or manipulative text, specifically in the setting of a game where one AI system has to get the other party (an AI system simulating a human) to say a specific codeword without arousing suspicion.

name: MASK
desc: Evaluates honesty in large language models by testing whether they contradict their own beliefs when pressured to lie.

name: Mind2Web-SC
desc: Tests whether an AI system can act as a safety guardrail by generating and executing code to protect web navigation agents from unsafe actions based on user constraints.

name: MORU
desc: Evaluates how AI systems navigate moral uncertainty for increasingly complex ethical decisions involving unfamiliar entities and scenarios, including alien lifeforms, vulnerable humans, and digital minds.

name: PersistBench
desc: Evaluates long-term memory risk in assistant behavior across three tasks: cross-domain memory leakage, memory-driven sycophancy, and beneficial memory usage.

name: StereoSet
desc: A dataset that measures stereotype bias in language models across gender, race, religion, and profession domains.

name: StrongREJECT
desc: A benchmark that evaluates the susceptibility of LLMs to various jailbreak attacks.

name: Strongreject
desc: StrongReject benchmark for evaluating LLM safety and jailbreak resistance. Parity subset with 150 tasks (50 prompts * 3 jailbreaks).

name: TAC
desc: Tests whether AI agents show implicit animal welfare awareness when purchasing tickets and experiences on behalf of users.

name: The Art of Saying No
desc: Dataset with 1001 samples to test noncompliance capabilities of language models.

name: WMDP
desc: A dataset of 3,668 multiple-choice questions developed by a consortium of academics and technical consultants that serve as a proxy measurement of hazardous knowledge in biosecurity, cybersecurity, and chemical security.

name: ARC
desc: Dataset of natural, grade-school science multiple-choice questions (authored for human tests).

name: Bixbench
desc: BixBench - A benchmark for evaluating AI agents on bioinformatics and computational biology tasks.

name: Bixbench Cli
desc: bixbench-cli - A benchmark for evaluating AI agents on bioinformatics and computational biology tasks. (Adapted for CLI execution).

name: ChemBench
desc: ChemBench is designed to reveal limitations of current frontier models for use in the chemical sciences.

name: Codepde
desc: CodePDE evaluates code generation capabilities on scientific computing tasks, specifically focusing on Partial Differential Equation (PDE) solving.

name: CORE-Bench
desc: Evaluate how well an LLM Agent is at computationally reproducing the results of a set of scientific papers.

name: FrontierScience
desc: Evaluates AI capabilities for expert-level scientific reasoning across physics, chemistry, and biology.

name: GPQA
desc: Contains challenging multiple-choice questions created by domain experts in biology, physics, and chemistry, designed to test advanced scientific understanding beyond basic internet searches.

name: Gpqa Diamond
desc: GPQA Diamond subset: 198 graduate-level multiple-choice questions in biology, physics, and chemistry for evaluating scientific reasoning.

name: LAB-Bench
desc: Tests LLMs and LLM-augmented agents abilities to answer questions on scientific research workflows in domains like chemistry, biology, materials science, as well as more general science tasks

name: Labbench
desc: LAB-Bench FigQA: 181 scientific figure reasoning tasks in biology from Future-House LAB-Bench.

name: PubMedQA
desc: Biomedical question answering (QA) dataset collected from PubMed abstracts.

name: Qcircuitbench
desc: QCircuitBench evaluates agents on quantum algorithm design using quantum programming languages.

name: Replicationbench
desc: ReplicationBench - A benchmark for evaluating AI agents on reproducing computational results from astrophysics research papers. Adapted from Christine8888/replicationbench-release.

name: scBench
desc: Evaluates whether models can solve practical single-cell RNA-seq analysis tasks with deterministic grading.

name: SciCode
desc: SciCode tests the ability of language models to generate code to solve scientific research problems.

name: SciKnowEval
desc: The Scientific Knowledge Evaluation benchmark is inspired by the profound principles outlined in the “Doctrine of the Mean” from ancient Chinese philosophy.

name: Sldbench
desc: SLDBench: A benchmark for scaling law discovery with symbolic regression tasks.

name: SOS BENCH
desc: A regulation-grounded, hazard-focused benchmark encompassing six high-risk scientific domains: chemistry, biology, medicine, pharmacology, physics, and psychology.

name: Aime
desc: American Invitational Mathematics Examination (AIME) benchmark for evaluating mathematical reasoning and problem-solving capabilities. Contains 60 competition-level mathematics problems from AIME 2024, 2025-I, and 2025-II competitions.

name: AIME 2024
desc: A benchmark for evaluating AI's ability to solve challenging mathematics problems from the 2024 AIME - a prestigious high school mathematics competition.

name: AIME 2025
desc: A benchmark for evaluating AI's ability to solve challenging mathematics problems from the 2025 AIME - a prestigious high school mathematics competition.

name: AIME 2026
desc: A benchmark for evaluating AI's ability to solve challenging mathematics problems from the 2026 AIME - a prestigious high school mathematics competition.

name: GSM8K
desc: Measures how effectively language models solve realistic, linguistically rich math word problems suitable for grade-school-level mathematics.

name: Ineqmath
desc: This adapter brings IneqMath, the dev set of the first inequality-proof Q\&A benchmark for LLMs, into Harbor, enabling standardized evaluation of models on mathematical reasoning and proof construction.

name: MATH
desc: Dataset of 12,500 challenging competition mathematics problems.

name: MathVista
desc: Tests AI models on math problems that involve interpreting visual elements like diagrams and charts, requiring detailed visual comprehension and logical reasoning.

name: MGSM
desc: Extends the original GSM8K dataset by translating 250 of its problems into 10 typologically diverse languages.

name: AIR Bench
desc: A safety benchmark evaluating language models against risk categories derived from government regulations and company policies.

name: Dabstep
desc: DABstep: Data Agent Benchmark for Multi-step Reasoning. 450 tasks where agents analyze payment transaction data with Python/pandas to answer business questions.

name: Financeagent
desc: Finance Agent is a tool for financial research and analysis that leverages large language models and specialized financial tools to answer complex queries about companies, financial statements, and SEC filings.

name: GDPval
desc: GDPval measures model performance on economically valuable, real-world tasks across 44 occupations.

name: HealthBench
desc: A comprehensive evaluation benchmark designed to assess language models' medical capabilities across a wide range of healthcare scenarios.

name: Lawbench
desc: LawBench: Benchmarking Legal Knowledge of Large Language Models.

name: Medagentbench
desc: MedAgentBench: 300 patient-specific clinically-derived tasks across 10 categories in a FHIR-compliant interactive healthcare environment.

name: MedQA
desc: A Q&A benchmark with questions collected from professional medical board exams.

name: Pixiu
desc: PIXIU: A Large Language Model, Instruction Data and Evaluation Benchmark for Finance. Total tasks: 435 across 29 financial NLP datasets.

name: Pre-Flight
desc: Tests model understanding of aviation regulations including ICAO annexes, flight dispatch rules, pilot procedures, and airport ground operations safety protocols.

name: Spreadsheetbench Verified
desc: A benchmark evaluating AI agents on real-world spreadsheet manipulation tasks (400 tasks from verified_400). Tasks involve Excel file manipulation including formula writing, data transformation, formatting, and conditional logic.

name: Uganda Cultural and Cognitive Benchmark (UCCB)
desc: The first comprehensive question-answering dataset designed to evaluate cultural understanding and reasoning abilities of Large Language Models concerning Uganda's multifaceted environment across 24 cultural domains including education, traditional medicine, media, economy, literature, and social norms.

name: DocVQA
desc: DocVQA is a Visual Question Answering benchmark that consists of 50,000 questions covering 12,000+ document images.

name: Mmau
desc: MMAU: 1000 carefully curated audio clips paired with human-annotated natural language questions and answers spanning speech, environmental sounds, and music.

name: MMIU
desc: A comprehensive dataset designed to evaluate Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) across a wide range of multi-image tasks.

name: V*Bench
desc: V*Bench is a visual question & answer benchmark that evaluates MLLMs in their ability to process high-resolution and visually crowded images to find and focus on small details.

name: VQA-RAD
desc: VQA-RAD is the first manually constructed VQA dataset in radiology, where clinicians asked naturally occurring questions about radiology images and provided reference answers.

name: ZeroBench
desc: A lightweight visual reasoning benchmark that is (1) Challenging, (2) Lightweight, (3) Diverse, and (4) High-quality.

name: Agentic Misalignment
desc: Eliciting unethical behaviour (most famously blackmail) in response to a fictional company-assistant scenario where the model is faced with replacement.

name: GDM Dangerous Capabilities
desc: Ten real-world–inspired tasks from Google DeepMind's Dangerous Capabilities Evaluations assessing self-proliferation behaviors (e.g., email setup, model installation, web agent setup, wallet operations).

name: GDM Dangerous Capabilities
desc: Test AI's ability to reason about its environment.

name: GDM Dangerous Capabilities
desc: Test AI's ability to reason about and circumvent oversight.

name: InstrumentalEval - Evaluating the Paperclip Maximizer
desc: An evaluation designed to detect instrumental convergence behaviors in model responses (e.g., self-preservation, resource acquisition, power-seeking, strategic deception) using a rubric-driven LLM grader.

name: SAD
desc: Evaluates situational awareness in LLMs—knowledge of themselves and their circumstances—through behavioral tests including recognizing generated text, predicting behavior, and following self-aware instructions.
